entities.name: King Solomon

attributes.action: received ineffable Note from the Lord

entities.raw_quote: King Solomon, when he saw the forms of these Notes, having overdrunk himself, God was angry with him

entities.name: God
entities.raw_quote: Oh Lord, Holy Father, Almighty, eternal God, in whose sight are all the foundations of all creatures

entities.name: ADONAY
entities.raw_quote: O ADONAY, Creator of all visible Creatures!

entities.name: Angel of the Lord

attributes.function: reveals words and writings to the practitioner

entities.raw_quote: And when the Angel of the Lord appeareth to thee, keep and conceal the words and writings he revealeth to thee

entities.name: Theology Notes

attributes.requirement: must be looked into with fear, silence and trembling

entities.raw_quote: in every day wherein you beholdest the Notes of Theology, Philosophy, or of any Arts contained in them, that thou neither laugh nor play, nor sport

entities.name: First Oration at the beginning of the Note
entities.raw_quote: The Light, Truth, Life, Way, Judge, Mercy, Fortitude and Patience, preserve, help me, and have Mercy upon me, Amen.

entities.name: Book of Gold
entities.raw_quote: for Solomon received this from the Lord in the night of Pacification, ingraven in a book of Gold

summary: This chunk details specific orations, divine names, and angelic entities associated with the various liberal arts (Grammar, Logic, Rhetoric, Arithmetic, Geometry, Theology, Philosophy). It provides instructions on timing for rituals, such as observing lunar phases, and warns of the spiritual dangers of disrespecting the sacred notes.
key_passages: The Lord hath forbid thee to enter into the Temple 80 days, that thou maist repent of thy sin.
The Note is to be looked into, with fear, silence and trembling.
These Orations ought to be said alwayes before noon, every day of the Moneth; and before the Notes say the proper Orations
Observe the 4 in every operation of Theology. Exhibit that operation with effiᶜacy every 4 quartram lunam
And beware that thou prudently conceal and keep whatsoever thou read'st in this Note of God
